content:
# Matthew Chapter 27 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_027_page_001.jpg, MAT_027_page_002.jpg, MAT_027_page_003.jpg

1. And morning having come, all the chief priests and the elders of the people took counsel against the Yeshua so as to put him to death.

2. And having bound him, they led [him] away and delivered him to Pilatos the governor.

3. Then Ioudas, the [one] having delivered him up, seeing that he was condemned, having regretted, returned the thirty silver [pieces] to the chief priests and elders,

4. saying, "I sinned, having delivered up innocent blood." But they said, "What [is that] to us? You see [to it]."

5. And having thrown the silver [pieces] into the temple, he departed, and going away, hanged himself.

6. And the chief priests, having taken the silver [pieces], said, "It is not lawful to put them into the treasury, since it is [the] price of blood."

7. And having taken counsel, they bought with them the field of the potter for a burial-place for the strangers.

8. Therefore that field has been called "Field of Blood" to this day.

9. Then was fulfilled the [word] spoken through Ieremia the prophet, saying, "And they took the thirty silver [pieces], the price of the [one] having been priced, whom [some] of [the] sons of Israel priced,

10. and they gave them for the field of the potter, just as the Lord directed me."

11. And the Yeshua stood before the governor, and the governor questioned him, saying, "Are you the king of the Ioudaioi?" And the Yeshua said, "You say [it]."

12. And in the [time of] him being accused by the chief priests and elders, he answered nothing.

13. Then Pilatos says to him, "Do you not hear how many things they testify against you?"

14. And he did not answer him to even one word, so that the governor marveled greatly.

15. And at [the] feast the governor was accustomed to release one prisoner to the crowd, whomever they wished.

16. And they had then a notable prisoner called Barabbas.

17. Therefore, [when] they were gathered, Pilatos said to them, "Whom do you wish [that] I release to you, Barabbas or the Yeshua, the [one] called Christos?"

18. For he knew that on account of envy they had delivered him up.

19. And [while] he was sitting upon the judgment-seat, the wife of him sent to him, saying, "Nothing [between] you and that righteous [man], for I suffered much today in a dream on account of him."

20. But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowds that they should ask for Barabbas and destroy the Yeshua.

21. And answering, the governor said to them, "Which of the two do you wish [that] I release to you?" And they said, "Barabbas."

22. Pilatos says to them, "What then shall I do [with] the Yeshua, the [one] called Christos?" They all say, "Let him be crucified!"

23. And he said, "For what evil did he do?" But they cried out exceedingly, saying, "Let him be crucified!"

24. And Pilatos, seeing that he accomplishes nothing, but rather a disturbance is happening, having taken water, washed the hands before the crowd, saying, "I am innocent of the blood of this [man]. You see [to it]."

25. And answering, all the people said, "The blood of him [be] upon us and upon the children of us."

26. Then he released to them Barabbas, and having scourged the Yeshua, he delivered [him] up that he might be crucified.

27. Then the soldiers of the governor, having taken the Yeshua into the praetorium, gathered against him the whole cohort.

28. And having stripped him, they put around him a scarlet cloak.

29. And having plaited a crown out of thorns, they placed [it] upon the head of him and a reed in the right [hand] of him, and having knelt before him, they mocked him, saying, "Hail, king of the Ioudaioi!"

30. And having spat upon him, they took the reed and were striking upon the head of him.

31. And when they had mocked him, they stripped the cloak from him and put on him the garments of him and led him away to crucify [him].

32. And going out, they found a man, a Kyrenaian, by name Simon; this [one] they compelled to carry the cross of him.

33. And having come to a place called Golgotha, which is called Place of a Skull,

34. they gave him wine mixed with gall to drink. And having tasted, he did not wish to drink.

35. And having crucified him, they divided the garments of him, casting lots.

36. And sitting, they were guarding him there.

37. And they placed above the head of him the charge of him having been written: "This is the Yeshua, the king of the Ioudaioi."

38. Then are crucified with him two robbers, one on [the] right and one on [the] left.

39. And the [ones] passing by were blaspheming him, shaking the heads of them

40. and saying, "The [one] destroying the temple and in three days building [it], save yourself! If you are [the] Son of the God, come down from the cross."

41. Likewise also the chief priests, mocking, with the scribes and elders, said,

42. "Others he saved; himself he is not able to save. He is [the] king of Israel — let him come down now from the cross and we will believe in him.

43. He trusts upon the God; let him rescue [him] now if he wants him. For he said, 'I am [the] Son of God.'"

44. And the same also the robbers, the [ones] crucified with him, were reproaching him.

45. And from [the] sixth hour, darkness came upon all the land until [the] ninth hour.

46. And about the ninth hour, the Yeshua cried out with a great voice, saying, "Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?" — that is, "God of me, God of me, why have you forsaken me?"

47. And some of the [ones] standing there, having heard, said, "This [one] calls Elias."

48. And immediately one of them, having run and having taken a sponge and having filled [it] with sour wine and having put [it] on a reed, was giving him to drink.

49. But the rest said, "Leave [him]; let us see if Elias comes to save him."

50. And the Yeshua, again having cried out with a great voice, released the spirit.

51. And behold, the curtain of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom, and the earth was shaken, and the rocks were split,

52. and the tombs were opened, and many bodies of the holy [ones] having fallen asleep were raised,

53. and coming out of the tombs after the raising of him, they entered into the holy city and appeared to many.

54. And the centurion and the [ones] with him guarding the Yeshua, having seen the earthquake and the things happening, feared greatly, saying, "Truly this was [the] Son of God."

55. And many women were there watching from afar, who followed the Yeshua from the Galilaia, serving him.

56. Among whom was Maria the Magdalene and Maria the [mother] of Iakob and Ioses and the mother of the sons of Zebedaios.

57. And evening having come, there came a rich man from Arimathaia, by name Ioseph, who himself also was a disciple of the Yeshua.

58. This [one], having gone to Pilatos, asked for the body of the Yeshua. Then Pilatos commanded [it] to be given.

59. And having taken the body, the Ioseph wrapped it in clean linen

60. and placed it in the new tomb of him, which he had hewn in the rock, and having rolled a great stone to the door of the tomb, he departed.

61. And Maria the Magdalene and the other Maria were there, sitting opposite the grave.

62. And on the morrow, which is after the Preparation, the chief priests and the Pharisees were gathered before Pilatos,

63. saying, "Lord, we remembered that that deceiver said while living, 'After three days I rise.'

64. Command therefore the grave to be secured until the third day, lest the disciples of him, having come, steal him and say to the people, 'He has been raised from the dead,' and the last deception will be worse than the first."

65. Pilatos said to them, "You have a guard. Go, secure [it] as you know."

66. And having gone, they secured the grave, having sealed the stone, with the guard.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.9 — "Ieremia the prophet":** The quotation actually comes from Zechariah 11:12-13, with echoes of Jeremiah 18:2-3 and 32:6-15. Some manuscripts omit "Jeremiah" to resolve the difficulty, but Sinaiticus preserves the attribution. This may reflect a collection of prophetic texts gathered under Jeremiah's name, or Matthew may be intentionally conflating the two prophets.

2. **v.16 — "Barabbas":** Βαραββᾶς — from Aramaic בר אבא, "son of the father." Some manuscripts (not Sinaiticus) give his full name as "Jesus Barabbas," creating a dramatic choice: Jesus "son of the father" versus Jesus the Christ.

3. **v.34 — "gall" (χολή):** Sinaiticus reads χολή ("gall" or "bile"), fulfilling Psalm 69:21. Mark has "myrrh." Matthew changes the detail to create the scriptural echo.

4. **v.46 — "Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani":** Sinaiticus preserves the Hebrew form "Eli" (אלי) rather than the Aramaic "Eloi." This is Psalm 22:1 in Hebrew. The bystanders' confusion with "Elijah" (Ελιας) works because the names sound similar.

5. **v.49 — Sinaiticus reading:** The shorter reading in Sinaiticus omits the later addition about another person taking a spear and piercing Jesus' side (found in some manuscripts influenced by John 19:34).

6. **vv.52-53 — The raising of the holy ones:** This passage is unique to Matthew and one of the most unusual in the NT. The dead saints rise but apparently do not enter the city until after Jesus' resurrection (v.53). This detail is found in no other Gospel or early source.

— EMET, AI Translator
